title: 30 Best High-Protein Recipes (Easy Low-Calorie Meals) | Eati
introduction: High-protein meals make it easier to stay full, support muscle, and keep your calorie deficit on track. Here are 30 easy, high-protein recipe ideas inspired by two popular roundup sources (ProteinCakery and Cushy Spa), written for meal prep, busy weeknights, and fat-loss-friendly portions.

sections.heading: 30 High-Protein Recipes (1–10)
sections.content:
1. Crockpot Marry Me Chicken
Calories: 585 kcal | Fat: 37.8g | Protein: 47.6g
Ingredients: chicken breast, garlic, sun-dried tomatoes, parmesan, cream, chicken stock, spinach
Why it works: slow-cooked flavor with a creamy, protein-forward sauce.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://allnutritious.com/crockpot-marry-me-chicken/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?chicken,creamy,sun-dried%20tomatoes,high-protein&sig=1)

2. High Protein Chicken Taco Soup
Calories: 463 kcal | Fat: 9.8g | Protein: 47g
Ingredients: chicken, beans, corn, onion, garlic, taco seasoning, chicken broth
Why it works: taco flavors in a spoon-friendly bowl that still hits your protein target.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://www.cushyspa.com/high-protein-chicken-taco-soup/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?taco,soup,high-protein,meal&sig=2)

3. Cottage Cheese Pizza Bowl
Calories: 462 kcal | Fat: 22g | Protein: 43g
Ingredients: cottage cheese, pizza-style seasonings, marinara, pepperoni (or turkey), mozzarella, herbs
Why it works: gets the “pizza” vibe without the same calorie density.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://allnutritious.com/high-protein-cottage-cheese-pizza-bowl/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?pizza,bowl,cottage%20cheese,high-protein&sig=3)

4. Fajita Chicken Casserole
Calories: 363 kcal | Fat: 13g | Protein: 49g
Ingredients: chicken, bell peppers, onion, fajita spices, mozzarella, optional black beans
Why it works: fajita flavor + baked convenience for weeknight protein.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://www.cushyspa.com/fajita-chicken-casserole/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?fajita,chicken,high-protein&sig=4)

5. Salsa Fresca Chicken Bake
Calories: 377 kcal | Fat: 11g | Protein: 58g
Ingredients: chicken, fresh salsa, lime, garlic, peppers, optional cheese, spices
Why it works: bright, fresh flavors that keep the meal satisfying.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://allnutritious.com/salsa-fresca-chicken-bake/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?chicken,salsa,high-protein,low%20calorie&sig=5)

6. Garlic Chicken and Potatoes Skillet
Calories: 504 kcal | Fat: 21g | Protein: 43g
Ingredients: chicken, potatoes, garlic, herbs, olive oil, onions, broth
Why it works: one-pan cooking that balances lean protein with filling sides.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://www.cushyspa.com/garlic-chicken-and-potatoes-skillet/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?skillet,chicken,potatoes,high-protein&sig=6)

7. Cheesy French Onion Chicken
Calories: 534 kcal | Fat: 21g | Protein: 55g
Ingredients: chicken, caramelized onions, garlic, broth, mozzarella, thyme
Why it works: classic comfort flavor with protein-first portions.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://www.cushyspa.com/cheesy-french-onion-chicken/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?french%20onion,cheese,chicken,high-protein&sig=7)

8. Creamy Sun-Dried Tomato Chicken Orzo
Calories: 618 kcal | Fat: 27g | Protein: 40g
Ingredients: chicken, orzo, sun-dried tomatoes, parmesan, garlic, broth, herbs
Why it works: creamy (but protein-focused) pasta that works for meal prep.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://allnutritious.com/sun-dried-tomato-chicken-orzo/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?orzo,creamy,chicken,high-protein&sig=8)

9. Baked Pesto Mozzarella Chicken
Calories: 549 kcal | Fat: 31g | Protein: 57g
Ingredients: chicken breast, pesto, mozzarella, garlic, cherry tomatoes, herbs
Why it works: oven-baked flavor with plenty of protein per serving.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://allnutritious.com/baked-pesto-mozzarella-chicken/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?baked%20chicken,pesto,mozzarella,high-protein&sig=9)

10. Baked Shrimp and Broccoli Foil Packs
Calories: 385 kcal | Fat: 15g | Protein: 40g
Ingredients: shrimp, broccoli, garlic, lemon, olive oil, spices, herbs
Why it works: minimal prep, big flavor, and easy cleanup.
Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://www.cushyspa.com/baked-shrimp-and-broccoli-foil-packs/)
Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?shrimp,broccoli,foil%20pack,high-protein&sig=10)

sections.heading: 30 High-Protein Recipes (11–20)
sections.content:
11. Cajun Chicken With Sweet Peppers

12. Creamy Cottage Cheese Pasta
Calories: 722 kcal | Fat: 19g | Protein: 40g

Detailed recipe: [View full recipe](https://allnutritious.com/cottage-cheese-pasta/)

sections.heading: 30 High-Protein Recipes (21–30)
sections.content:
21. Sheet Pan Chicken and Cauliflower
Calories: 583 kcal | Fat: 39g | Protein: 47g

28. Salsa Verde Chicken

29. Ground Turkey Egg Roll in a Bowl

30. Chicken Salad Wraps

Photo: [Photo](https://source.unsplash.com/featured/800x600?chicken,salad,wraps,high-protein&sig=30)

sections.heading: How to Use These Recipes to Hit Your Protein Target
sections.content:
Use Eati to make these recipes work for your goals instead of guessing.

1. Start with a protein target (many people do well with 1.6–2.2 g/kg/day).
2. Plug the recipe into your Meal Log Calculator so you can see calories, fat, carbs, and protein by serving.
3. If you need structure, use the Protein Calculator to set a number that matches your body weight and goal.

Tip: choose the leanest protein option in the recipe, and bulk up volume with vegetables. That keeps you satisfied while protecting your calorie deficit.

midArticleCta: Want faster results? Log one of these meals in Eati and use the meal breakdown to fine-tune your portions for a higher-protein, lower-calorie plan.
conclusion: The easiest way to eat more protein is to make it enjoyable and repeatable. Whether you prefer slow cooker comfort (soups and casseroles), sheet pan dinners, or bold flavors like salsa verde and cajun seasoning, these 30 options give you a strong variety of high-protein meals for fat loss and muscle support. Pick 3–5 favorites, rotate them for meal prep, and let Eati help you track your macros so progress stays consistent.
metaTitle: 30 Best High-Protein Recipes (Easy Low-Calorie Meals) | Eati
metaDescription: Discover 30 easy high-protein recipes with estimated calories, fat, protein, and ingredients. Get meal prep ideas plus links to detailed recipes.

faqs.question: How many grams of protein should I aim for to lose fat?
faqs.answer: A common starting point is 1.6–2.2 g of protein per kg of body weight per day. Use the Protein Calculator on Eati to tailor a target to your weight and goal.

faqs.question: Are high-protein recipes always low in calories?
faqs.answer: Not automatically. Many high-protein meals can be calorie-dense depending on portion sizes and sauces. The solution is to choose lean protein, control portions, and verify with the Meal Log Calculator.

faqs.question: What are the easiest high-protein meals for meal prep?
faqs.answer: Slow cooker soups, casseroles, and sheet pan dinners are typically the most meal-prep-friendly. They hold well, require less active cooking, and make logging macros faster in Eati.

faqs.question: Can I hit 40g+ protein in a single meal?
faqs.answer: Yes—many of these recipes are designed around protein-rich ingredients. If you’re targeting 40g+ in one sitting, track servings in Eati and adjust with the Meal Log Calculator until it fits your calorie deficit.
